proof_sketch: The removed __syncthreads() occurs after the mean is computed from shared_sum[0] and before the variance computation. The last __syncthreads() in the reduction loop (line 126) ensures all writes to shared_sum are visible to all threads before any thread reads shared_sum[0] to compute the mean. Therefore, the barrier after the mean is redundant; no thread writes to shared_sum afterward, so removing it does not introduce any race condition or change the final result under any inputs. The fixed shape and any values produce bitwise identical outputs.
